Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.1 REST Signal
Requirement Summary: REST contributes up to 30 pts of the 100-point Health Score. The top tier (≥12.70 V mean rest voltage) maps to 30 pts (Excellent). Wrong scoring at this tier silently penalises healthy batteries and triggers false replacement forecasts.
Title: Verify scoring REST signal as 30 pts when 30-day mean rest_voltage_avg is ≥ 12.70 V
Preconditions:
• Test vehicle TLX-DEMO-001 exists in the Flespi tenant with ≥ 30 days of telemetry
• Calc 2805151 (Rest Voltage) is enabled and producing rest_voltage_avg samples
• 30-day mean rest_voltage_avg is engineered to 12.72 V
• Test user is logged in to Fleet Pulse
Steps (Action):
1. Open the Flespi calc 2805151 device messages and inspect the 30-day mean
2. Navigate to Fleet Pulse dashboard and locate the TLX-DEMO-001 row
3. Hover the Health Score chip to open the tooltip
4. Read the REST score in the tooltip
5. Trigger a manual score recalculation (or wait for the next cycle) and re-open the tooltip
Expected Result:
1. Sample count ≥ 20; visible mean reads 12.72 V (±0.01)
2. Row renders with Health Score chip visible
3. Tooltip opens; REST row visible
4. REST shows 30/30 with Excellent (green) indicator
5. REST remains 30/30 — score is stable across recalc
Test Data:
vehicle_id: TLX-DEMO-001
mean_rest_voltage_30d: 12.72 V
sample_count: 24
calc_id: 2805151
Postconditions:
• REST score persisted as 30 in score_history
• Overall battery_health_score reflects REST=30
• No fallback flag set on this score record
Notes: Score persistence assertion depends on recalc cadence — see Q2 (assumed ≤15 min).
Requirement ID: REQ-3.1.a — traced to §3.1 scoring thresholds table

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (Temperature compensation)
Requirement Summary: Temperature compensation: crank_comp = crank_obs + 0.025 × (25 − ambient °C). Required so cold-morning measurements don't false-fail healthy batteries.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation formula adds 0.475 V when raw crank is 9.6 V at 6 °C ambient
Preconditions:
• Vehicle's location at crank event time has WeatherAPI ambient = 6 °C
• Raw crank_dip captured by calc 2805150 = 9.60 V
Steps (Action):
1. Inspect raw crank_dip and ambient temp for the event
2. Inspect forecast_audit.compensated_crank_dip
3. Open tooltip
Expected Result:
1. Raw = 9.60 V at 6.0 °C
2. Equals 9.60 + 0.025×(25−6) = 10.075 V (±0.005)
3. CRANK = 25/25 Strong — would have been 13/25 without compensation
Notes: Hot-side compensation behaviour per Q16.

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (Temperature compensation)

1. Confirm raw crank_dip and ambient
2. Read forecast_audit.compensated_crank_dip
Expected Result:
1. 9.60 V at 25 °C
2. Equals 9.60 V (no adjustment)

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (Calculator config)
Requirement Summary: Regression: calc 2805150 must use min_active=0 with merge_message_before/after=true. Without these flags 99% of crank events are missed (April 28 incident).
Title: Verify calc 2805150 captures cranks correctly with min_active=0 and merge_message_before/after=true
Preconditions:
• Vehicle drove ≥ 5 ignition cycles in a test session
• Permission to modify calc 2805150 config in a sandbox tenant
Steps (Action):
1. Open calc 2805150 config in Flespi
2. Count ignition OFF→ON transitions in raw telemetry
3. Count crank events produced by calc 2805150
4. Toggle merge_message_before/after to false in sandbox and recount
5. Restore configuration to true
Expected Result:
1. min_active=0; merge_message_before=true; merge_message_after=true
2. ≥ 5 transitions
3. Matches transition count within ±1
4. Event count drops by ≥ 90% — confirms regression
5. Event count returns to Step 3 level
Postconditions: • Calc config restored to required values; production capture rate ≥ 95%
Notes: Reverts April 28 misconfiguration. Sandbox tenant only.
Requirement ID: REQ-3.2.c — traced to §3.2 Critical config fix (28 Apr)

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.3 TREND Signal (Regression x-axis fix)
Requirement Summary: Regression: x-axis MUST be (interval_begin + interval_end) / 2 NOT the calc emission timestamp. v2.0 had this wrong and masked BMW-class instability.

Notes: Strong regression candidate with frozen reference dataset.

1. Sort indicator appears
2. Red-badge rows at the top; amber next; green last
3. Order reverses correctly
Test Data:
vehicles: 10 mixed
expected_order: red-first then amber then green
Requirement ID: REQ-Status.f — traced to Status Column Spec (Sortability)

Screen / Section: Fleet Pulse: Health Score Tooltip Spec (Open/close interactions)

close: blur; mouseleave; Escape

3. Open tooltip on the red-row vehicle
4. Test exactly at the 40% boundary
5. Test exactly at the 80% boundary
Expected Result:
1. All 6 dots green (each signal ≥80%)
2. Each ≥40% and <80% renders amber
3. Each <40% renders red
4. Behaviour matches CQ-004 resolution
5. Green per spec
Postconditions: • Dot colours match thresholds at all percentages

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (WeatherAPI fallback)
Requirement Summary: NEW v4 coverage. When vehicle has no GPS fix at crank event WeatherAPI cannot resolve ambient temperature directly. Pipeline must fall back to last-known fix within 7 days.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation falls back to the most recent GPS fix within 7 days when current fix is missing
Preconditions:
• Vehicle has a crank event with no concurrent GPS fix
• Last known GPS fix was 3 days ago at a location whose WeatherAPI temp = 18 °C
Steps (Action):
1. Run the CRANK pipeline on the event
2. Inspect forecast_audit.temp_source
3. Inspect forecast_audit.ambient_temp_c
4. Inspect forecast_audit.compensated_crank_dip
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline runs
2. 'last_known_fix_within_7d'
3. 18 °C
4. Computed using 18 °C; not the 25 °C default
Notes: Closes review §4.10 gap (no GPS scenario).

Screen / Section: Battery Health & Replacement Forecast — §3.2 CRANK Signal (WeatherAPI fallback)
Requirement Summary: NEW v4 coverage. When no GPS fix exists in last 7 days either pipeline falls back to 25 °C baseline (zero adjustment) and logs a warning.
Title: Verify CRANK temperature compensation falls back to 25 °C baseline when no GPS fix exists within the last 7 days
Preconditions: • No GPS fix in the last 7 days for the vehicle
Steps (Action):
1. Run the CRANK pipeline on a crank event in this state
2. Inspect forecast_audit.temp_source
3. Inspect forecast_audit.ambient_temp_c
4. Inspect the compensation factor
5. Inspect forecast_audit.warnings
Expected Result:
1. Pipeline runs
2. 'baseline_default'
3. 25 °C
4. 0.025 × (25 − 25) = 0 (no adjustment)
5. Contains 'temp_fallback_baseline'
Notes: Closes review §4.10 gap (no fix within 7 d). Hot-side compensation clamp per Q16.
